title: Best buds for people with conch piercings
text:
This is the first set of earbuds that actually stay in my ear with my piercings. I have a big conch piercing as well as both tragus and I have tried to shake the buds out. They're not budging and I don't have to constantly readjust them. This alone is enough for me to give it 5 stars.

I am super impressed with the sound quality. Listening to O Fortuna was an epic experience. Noise cancellation provides for a great isolating experience and transparency is a welcomed option as well. Listening while being aware of your surroundings is always important and these do the job as long as you don't raise music level above maybe 75%. I haven't had it long enough to know how good the battery life is so I can't really speculate on that just yet but the ease of pairing actually shocked me.

I will say I'm not that fond of the casing. It feels a tad flimsy and it doesn't handle well. It is that type of material that could easily slip from your hand so I highly suggest a silicon

title: Great little earbuds, ANC is really good
text: I've millions of bluetooth/headphones that i use for a loud work environment so volume and ANC is a big deal. the only drawback with ANC is that drains the battery and no one pair can get me thru the day. So currently i also use the Jabra Elite Pro 7 and I just get these Beats Studio Buds. The Beats replaced JAbra Elite 75t AND my AirPods Pro Max. The Jabra 75t are just old and don't work as well and the AirPods which have great sound and call just don't stay in my ears so I actually had to buy the plastic covers that go on the AirPods so they lock in better. For the price they should just stay in. Also the AirPods battery is done to about 4 hours! The Beats Studio Buds are small, even though the case is huge! The price isn't bad at $150, the Jabra Pro 7 was $130. I've gotten almost 6 hours out of the battery but it is brand new. The sound quality is really good, I played Whole Lotta Love by Zeppelin and it sounded great. The ANC is great too, blocks out outside noise great. I have sma

title: Great wireless earbuds
text:
I've had my Beats Studio Buds for several months now and I am still thoroughly impressed with their performance. The sound quality is fantastic and the noise cancellation is top-notch. One of the standout features for me is the comfort. I can wear these earbuds for extended periods of time without any discomfort, which was a problem I faced with my wife's AirPods Pro. The earbuds fit securely in my ears and stay in place during my workouts or when I'm on the go. The compact charging case is also a plus and fits easily in my pocket.

Compared to the AirPods Pro, the Beats Studio Buds are a better value for the price. Not only do they sound better, but they are also more comfortable for extended listening. I would highly recommend these earbuds to anyone looking for a top-quality, comfortable, and stylish pair. The Beats Studio Buds are the perfect choice for anyone who wants high-quality sound in a convenient and comfortable package. Over several months of use, these earbuds h

title: BAD EAR FIT
text:
Got them on the Apple Store. The fit was terrible, I tried all the adaptors provided with the item but I decided to return them and buy the Airpads Pro. Those immediately fit my ear.
Again, every ear is different but the New Beats didn't perform at all for me in terms of fit.

title: Nice Sound, Great Design!
text:
For the last two years (May 2019 to June 2021) I've owned BOSE Sound Sports as my ear buds that I use at the gym. Sadly I lost them recently and needed a replacement. The Beats Studio Buds looked good and were a good price point. I was pleasantly surprised that the Beats were quite a bit better than the BOSE and at a better price point.

1. Sound: Just as good as the Bose Soundsports if not better, good volume amount.

2. Button Controls: Very nice light touch buttons on both the right and left buds. Unlike some other reviews that I have read pressing the buttons did NOT push the buds into the ear. I found the buttons to be a nice lite touch when compared to the SoundSports buttons, which were about 3 times harder to press (or more, really took some pressure).

3. CASE: Very well designed case, sleek, durable. Really liked the magnetic lid and charging ports, well designed and thought out.

4. BEATS APP: LIke that the app shows how

title: Not as good as my Airpods Pro, but MUCH more budget friendly for a backup pair.
text:
The charge on my AirPods Pro Buds doesn't last the entirety of my long-haul flights or overnight if I wear them to sleep. Buying a 2nd set of AirPods to always have a charged pair on hand seemed excessively expensive and indulgent. These Beats Studio Buds do the trick for less than 1/3 the price of my AirPods. For my purposes, the Beats Studio Buds work out great.
The sound quality and noise-cancelation features of the Beats Studio Buds are just a step down from my noise-canceling AirPods Pro Buds. With the Beats Studio Buds, I hear a little more of what's happening around me which is disappointing, hence the 3 stars. However, I wouldn't give the AirPods Pro noise canceling feature more than 4 stars. Neither brand of Buds holds a candle to my Beats over-the-ear noise-canceling headphones. For sound quality, I get some weird feedback if I'm laying down and the Beats Buds rub against my pillow, whereas I've never had that problem with the AirPods Pro, hence 4 stars for Beats Buds vs

title: Great sound quality but not workout approved
text: 3/22 update:: I have been using these headphones almost exclusively for the gym. Maybe these just aren't a good fit for my ear shape but the design of these earbuds is just awful. I've tried every single size variation the ear buds came with and these ear buds simply will not sit in my ears like they should. I am constantly adjusting the ear bud back into my ear so it won't fall out (falls out frequently while lifting weights). I can't stretch doing toe touches without them falling out. I can't lift weights without them falling out. I can't sit on the exercise bike without them falling out. The button on the ear buds is so incredibly sensitive that every single time I have to adjust it so it doesn't fall out that it causes whatever I'm listening to stop. So then I have to touch it again, which can cause it to fall out of place yet again no matter how gentle I am. These are the most frustrating ear buds I have ever owned. The sound quality is EXCELLENT! But the shape & design of the ear

title: Noise Cancelling Makes a Difference, But Still Not Perfect
text:
I'm literally sitting here writing this review while city workers are outside my apartment jackhammering the sidewalk. The noise cancellation doesn't make all that noise go away, but it softens the noise enough to the point that it's tolerable, especially with some music on. The noise cancelling on these buds is better than my other noise cancelling headphones from Soundcore., Granted those aren't the highest end headphones but they are over-the-ear and one might assume they are more noise isolating to begin with. but they're still not as good.

ETA: After using these for a week, I don't think I can keep them. First, there are compatibility issues; sometimes when I connect I only get one bud to work and the other is on but silent. The buds don't seem to be able to remember multiple connections well, so to get both buds working I have to go through a re-pairing process. And perhaps most importantly, the button placement has proven really frustrating. These buds don't fit my ad

title: Only have had one month and are already going bad!
text: [[VIDEOID:52d337d0632f34dfa8f4ccb380e661a4]] I’m highly dissatisfied! $100 purchase for a product to last one month.  Unfortunately I’m right outside the window for returning it.  Should have just bought a set of airpod pros for my teen daughter. As you can see in the video there is an issue with something.

title: Not great.
text:
Got these at half off to replace my AirPods that stopped working. Overall, these weren’t nearly as good as I was hoping based on the $150 price and relatively good reviews.

Pros:
They do fit very well and stay in easily. Really comfortable to wear and I haven’t had them fall out while moving around.

Battery life is fairly good, definitely outlasts my older 2nd gen AirPods

Noise cancelling is nice, not up to par with over ear headphones but that’s to be expected.

Cons:
Sound. Out of the box these really lacked bass. Everyone has different preferences here so maybe that’s just me. Figured this would be an easy fix through their equalizer. Nope. The Bose app doesn’t support these earbuds, only the more expensive variants. Pretty unacceptable to have a pair of earbuds at this price range be intentionally hindered this way. And yes, they are sealed correctly.

Connectivity. I use these with an iPhone and getting them reconnecte

title: Horrible
text: I cannot believe this product passed any kind of design or usability review. I had another brand of $30 buds that were worlds better than these. I couldn’t even pick them up with my fingers. They felt impossible to grip and once you got them out, good luck getting them back in. It was like playing with a rubix cube. No reason they shouldn’t just snap back into place instantly. AND there was a constant audible static sound with and without music playing. I needed these because of sensory processing issues and out of the package they just about gave me a panic attack. Total waste of time.

title: Love it & Hate it all at the same time
text:
Let’s start with…

Reasons to love it…
-Sheek & Wireless
-Quick Charge
-Doesn’t require both air buds to use
-Can control on either air buds
-Clear reception when on a call

Reasons to hate it:
-What’s with the short charging wire and NO adapter. Had to order one for compatibility of current charging stations.
-Air buds pop out when wearing (even when using the smallest plug)
-No volume control (have to control from phone)

title: Worst microphone in an earphone ever
text: Microphone was horrible. The recipient could not hear anything I said. Highly muffled. Had to disconnect Bluetooth to talk to them

title: Tapping/pushing issue. Update:  Cannot FaceTime
text:
UPDATE:: So I took away a star because these would not work on a FaceTime call!!! Will be returning!!

Got my pair today. They sound good, nice bass but had issues with tapping when it came to skipping a song or going back 2 or 3 songs. And really it’s more like a “push” than a “tap”. I thought that was kind of annoying. I have not figured out what the long press is. This is only the 2nd pair of wireless earbuds I’ve had (had a different brand - cheap) so I’m not sure if all earbuds have it or not but I would have been nice to have volume control. Overall, not bad.

title: Don’t stay in. Shut off every time I adjust.
text:
They drive me CRAZY. I was looking for a more comfortable alternative to AirPods, which they are. BUT they fall out as I move around, which is super annoying! I’ve tried all three size adaptors.

When I put them back in, or readjust as they get loose, they make an unnerving mechanical sound and whatever I’m listening to shuts off, as the controls are super sensitive and right where you need to hold on to get them in.

Not worth the money!

title: No charger block included
text: Over $100 and they can’t include an adapter for usb to usb-c, or charger block? Overpriced overbassed headphones with short play time

title: Even survived the washer and dryer!
text: Great product. Survived the washer and dryers just landed up in the filter in the very hot dryer after accidentally washing and using the dryer until I realized where the one eat bud was at. If this thing can last in that especially on the heavy duty setting, I’d have to recommend it. Even the sound quality sounds as good as ever before and after. Great bass and quality for a small ear bud.

title: Awesome, unless you sweat
text: At first, wow.  Sounds great, small simple design, super fast charging...  then I used them while working out.  Nope, after trying to force them to stay in while I was sweaty and icky, I gave up.

title: Love these earbuds!
text: I love these beats earbuds so much! It's awesome being able to go between noise cancellation and transparency for when I'm on bike rides. Also the battery life is really good I feel like I don't have to charge them too often. Gave my boyfriend a pair too and he probably uses them more than I use mine, they're just that good!

title: Stability
text: Headphones aren’t good for extreme workouts.

title: Battery life is too short
text: Good sound and comfortable. Battery life is too short for me. Lasts only a couple days.

title: Great look, But i expected more Bass from Beats
text: Great Look and price is pretty good; however Panasonic, Jabra 75t and a few other come in at a better price point and the sound quality is better.  These do not sound bad... just not as good as I expected... $150 isn't a lot for name brand earbuds, but these are worth $99 .  I've got Bose, Jabra and several other top brands, so price isn't the issue it's value.  and for $150... it just isn't a great value to me.  The fit is secure, but feels like it might fall out of your ear.

title: Hw are you supposed to charge these?
text: Something is missing. You can't charge these!!!!

title: Nice addition to the Beats wireless line
text:
These are a solid addition to the ever growing true wireless market. Although this is a Beats branded pair, it is markedly different from the Powerbeats Pro. First, it’s a smaller form factor both in the buds themselves and the case. The buds in particular are very tiny. See pic comparing to the AirPods Pro. It has no stem but the bud itself sticks out your ears a little more, so they are uncomfortable to lay on if you were to watch a movie in bed or so.
They look like bolts in your ears from afar. With that being said, they are comfortable for regular use, at least for me. Although the silicone tips are less pliant and soft compared to the Pro’s. Sound quality is where it shines, with a nice resonating low and crisp highs. Mids is where these fall short. Battery lasts pretty well, about 6 hours with ANC on, haven’t tested with them off but it claims to last up to 8 hours. Now the case I believe should have been a little smaller since it doesn’t house a wireless coil, so not sure

title: I prefer these over my Airpods for workouts and lawn work
text: Sound is great in my opinion. They hold a charge much longer than I expected. They fit snugly in my ear and really do a nice job of  canceling out much of the external noise which is nice when I am in a noisy room or mowing the lawn. One fell out when I was jogging one night through the neighborhood (probably due to excessive sweat) but all other times when I have ran, walked, mountain biked, kayaked, worked out at the gym, or done yard work, they have stayed in without issue. I would buy again without hesitation.

title: Very difficult to pair with multiple devices
text:
I LOVE the sound and fit of these Studio Buds. Fit, feel and sound better than my AirPods. But and its a BIG but they are very difficult to pair with multiple devices. Basically you must unpair with one device and then fight to get it to pair with another. Deal breaker to many who may want to switch from MacBook to phone o iPad. Mine won't do it. 3 modest of sound is great. Don't expect noise cancellation to be as effective as over the ear headset, but its pretty good.

Dang I really like these but wish they had figured out the multiple bluetooth thing.

title: Beats
text: I am thinking of returning the noise cancelation is horrible

title: I would keep looking if you haven't bought these yet
text: Overall the look is nice and it got us wanting a pair but now that I have some and I even got my girl friend a pair and we dislike them. When we talk on the phone we can hear ALL back ground noise I can hear other people in the back ground better then I can hear her. I can hear the lightest noises from paper moving to a pen drop ALL back ground noises are heard and makes for a terrible phone conversation.  If you are sitting and doing nothing and not moving they are good but once you start moving around or working out these are terrible. They got me because of the brand and looks but I'm not happy neither is my gf.

title: Fantasic ear buds for the price!
text:
I got these for my 9 year old son to go with his Pixel 5a phone. He raves about the sound quality and battery life. High praise indeed from a 9 year old!

He was able to pair the earbuds himself with the phone and it was completely hassle free. I've always thought Beats products were overrated and overpriced, but these ear buds are worth every penny, sound great, and the noise cancelling is great.

10/10 would buy again.

title: Lifesaver!!
text: I just got out of the hospital and used these while there which helped me sleep through all the noise and people in and out of the room.  They were phenomenal!!

title: Battery life is terrible
text: The battery life on these earbuds are substandard! the charge drops rapidly I'm lucky if I get 2 hours of solid play time

title: Just what I needed!
text:
I wanted to upgrade from my AirPods that hurt my ears to buds that can connect from my personal phone (iPhone) to my work cell phone (iPhone) and to my work laptop (PC). These are great! I can listen to music while I work and switch gears to work calls and zoom meetings. The best part for me - they don’t hurt my ears!

I wasn’t looking for anything for working out or for listening to loud bass so I cannot vouch for those areas. But for what I was looking for, these are awesome!

title: Great wireless headphones with good sound, range, and battery life
text: I bought these to replace my old Beats. Overall, I'm very happy with these. The fit is nice in my ears and they don't hurt or bother me even after hours of wearing them. The bluetooth range is pretty far and I can walk around my house without it disconnecting. The battery life is much better than other completely wireless headphones I've had previously. The only con I've had so far is the noise canceling setting seems loud to me.  Overall I'm very happy with these so far!

title: Good When They Worked
text:
I thought these ear buds were just OK even when they worked like they were supposed to. I loved the compatibility between Android and iOS. I carry an iPhone and an Android phone for work and these really do switch back and forth well.
I really disliked the buttons. I kept accidentally hitting the buttons all the time.
I loved how loud they were.
I thought the ANC was just OK.
The transparency mode was not good at all.
Then I noticed when the battery level of the ear buds would drop under 50% I would start getting drop outs and static sounds in the right ear bud.
My experience was not great. I can't recommend these ear buds myself. They are not awful but there are better for the same amount or less money.

title: They’re Okay
text: Definitely just go with apple earbuds instead, they’re the same price basically anyway. Had em about a week. Case fell out of my sons pocket, he picked it up, one went flying out. Never did find it. Now we just have 1. The fall was approximately 2 feet as he was sitting in his chair in the desk at school.

title: 3/5… battery is good,sound quality meh
text:
Great cheap buy
Battery life is its best redeeming quality, next is it’s sturdiness cause I’ve dropped the buds a few times with no ill affect, noise canceling is nice but wavers harshly in loud places or where there’s lots of commotion. Sound quality is great in a quiet place but it’s not good for moving Or laughing, a slight twinge and it starts slipping right out

title: You MUST recharge these buds every day!
text: These buds work well  & I'm not an expert with the noise cancellation. I did have problems with them charging. I'm used to giving my buds short time charging as I like to deplete the case battery before charging again. Apparently these buds like to have a full charge daily without fail. Ever since I've offered this rule I've not had any lack of power issues.

title: Cheap, compressed, bassless audio from a $25 pair of cheap buds
text: I am absolutely blown away at the low quality of these earbuds. I have an audio engineering degree and own some $2500 headphones including Stax headphones and a pair of lovely flagship AKG 701 headphones... in addition to Amphion & Barefoot monitors. These are by far the cheapest, most lifeless earbudds I've ever experienced and are below my $50 cheap in ear buds I replaced these with. Brand apparently means everything to half asleep American consumers because these are shockingly bad and yet they have 5 star reviews. Sheesh. I am blow away actually, at how poor the mid range and bass response on these are. Thin, hyper compressed, small soundstage, boxed in and completely lifeless. The audio is poorly compressed and missing a massive amount of frequency range needed to minimally enjoy music. I do not recommend this product to anyone looking for quality. I suspect Apple bought this company and the average unsuspecting consumer has no idea the difference between quality and "loudness" of

title: Garbage
text: The Bluetooth quality is poor cuts out all the time, the on and off controls are hyper sensitive, and the noise canceling and transparency Moses don't even work.  I have purchased other earbuds for a fraction of the price and the overall quality was better.  I expected more from Beats.

title: Brain fog eye tiredness
text:
I used them for few hrs while I slept I use airpods no issue my fav but I used these for few hrs with noise canceling to just rain sounds.
Woke up very tired eyes super dry mouth lots of radiation prob very cheap bad noice canceling messes with your brain body beware.

title: Mediocre you’re just paying for the name
text: I bought these because I thought they would be good sound quality these are just mediocre headphones you’re better off with the Amazon echo buds they have better sound quality and better noise cancellation than this the touch button is very sensitive if you’re trying to adjust it in your ear while on a call good luck because it will end the call

title: Pay for the name
text: They're good but so are the skull candy for half the price. Sounds about the same to me

title: Nice sound. Easy to connect.
text: Nice sound and Noise Cancellation options on the device. Easy to connect to Apple. Comfortable but not for all ears. They fell out of mine - I seem to be between ear tip sizes. That’s genetics, not Beats’ fault - no stars removed for incompatible ear holes. If they’re for a child - buy a lanyard. Easy to drop when taking out of the ears. If they stay in your ears, excellent choice. Get a Amazon Warehouse deal for Like New if you can - up to half off retail price - no ear tip replacements required.

title: Daughter loves these!
text: Since I have a teenager, I’m not sure if she’s ignoring me or if the noise cancellation really works. Lol. But these are great. I bought her the Apple ones a couple years ago and all she did was complain about my near $300 purchase! She said these were WAY BETTER! She likes the fit in her ear, the sound, and the noise cancellation. She complained about the sensitivity of the Apple ones but she said these are much better as she doesn’t accidentally hang up on ppl like she did with the others. Her review to me so so great, I bought myself a pair in gray and I totally agree with her! Love them!

title: worst earphones ever
text: They are uncomfortable, have muffled noise, do not connect with devices well, and costumer services is BAD

title: Not worth aggravation!
text: One ear bud refused to play sound. Discovered this was extremely common. Spent a half hour troubleshooting, when I could’ve been working on my current novel. Tried every fix suggested online in multiple forums and videos. I have NOT had this problem with $20 cheap wireless buds and certainly not with $200 Bose. I’d sworn off Beats years ago due to bad quality, but gave them another try.  I regret it. It’s not worth the aggravation, let alone the hassle of returning defective earbuds. No excuse at this price.

title: Excellent!!!
text: Sound is great. Won’t fall off as iPods Pro. Good quality great price. Don’t have problems with calls. Would buy again and do recommend!

title: Fits great in my ear! Great sound!
text: Loved the way the bud sits snug in my ear. The music quality is awesome

title: Extraordinary Sound
text: My first Dre Beats ear buds (with wires) are about 15 years old and still producing amazing quality sound, as is my Beats Pill. I knew when I wanted wireless buds that Beats would be the brand.  I’m not disappointed.  With three sizes of bud cushions included, you’ll find the perfect fit for comfort and noise cancellation.  Compact case doubles as a charger and a single charge can provide up to 24 hours listening time.  Nothing equals the sound quality, sorry AirPods, and if you use an iPhone you’re already set up for Beats.  Just open the case and device detection pops up on your phone.  There aren’t even “instructions” as you might expect, because you don’t need them.  If you do, you’re directed to the Dre Beats website where you’ll easily find your answer.  From box to listening in zero seconds.  Buy with confidence.

title: No instructions, have to use the app, sound quality only okay
text:
These are overall perfectly okay earbuds, but they certainly don't outcompete some of the similar ones on the market.

I have a set of Bose earbuds, and a Jabra set that I like better.

When I think about beats studio buds, I think about sound quality, and particularly I think about Dr. Dre, and playing some music with a thumping beat. Well, the sound quality on this was not great for listening to music. It's perfectly fine for a podcast, but music felt muted and unexciting through these.

The noise cancellation did work well, so I was impressed with that. And they are very comfortable to wear.

But another thing he didn't really care for with these was that there is no instructions, and there's no way to pair these with my phone without downloading their app. I really don't always want to be forced into using their dedicated app. I appreciate that the app has some settings that are very useful, but I would prefer that be an option rather than a

title: WOW
text: Very great buy for the buck. My wife has the Apple Earbuds. I see no sound quality deafferents.

title: Great sound.  Battery could be better
text: I really like my beats earbuds. The only thing that could be better is the battery life.

title: Great sound, customizable and easy to use. What is there not to like
text: These Beats earbuds provide wonderful sound for an ear bud. The sound is full and seems unlimited, other earbuds I have used seem to die off in areas of songs where the sounds reach towards the limits. These are easy to fit and use, it does take getting used to the one button operation, but it is not a factor really. You will also get an app to control and customize your ear buds, handy in setting up these for exactly how you want them to work. In all a really good set of ear buds, you cannot go wrong with these.

title: Best Earbuds !!! Highly recommend to Motorcyclist!!!
text:
I can’t tell you how many pairs of earbuds I have purchased!! These are by far the very best ones!! The sound quality is awesome and I have loud pipes. I can still hear my music perfectly!! They fit snuggly in my ears as well!!
Highly recommend to motorcyclists!!!

title: Won’t stay in my ears!
text: I was excited about these until i realized they won’t stay in my ears. I’ve tried every size ear piece that comes with the earbuds and I’m constantly having to shove them back in. No way i can workout in these.

title: My son Loves these
text: Great sound. My son Loves them.

title: Good sound but don't compare to Airpods
text:
One of my Airpods died, so I started using my wife's "Beats" earbuds. Overall they do an OK job, but have a few shortcomings compared to the Apple earbuds.

Positive: Strong base sound, very good noise cancellation, OK sound overall. They pair OK with iPhone after initial setup.

Negative: Much weaker Bluetooth range compared to Airpods, poor positioning of controls results in stopping play whenever you push earbuds into ear; don't work well with Apple watch (i.e. whenever you use controls on earbud, they disconnect from Apple watch control). They are also a bit bulkier but that may not be a big issue.

Summary: If you are going to lay out the bucks for good earbuds anyway, spend a few extra and get the Apple product. Just DON'T drop an Airpod into sink water :(

title: Good purchase/would recommend
text: Good sound quality and great price point vs my dog destroyed Apple earbuds. I would buy again

title: Can’t turn the volume up high enough
text: It seems as though there’s some sort of volume level ceiling that I am unable to change. They are my eardrums so if I wanna blow them out on some Lynyrd Skynyrd shouldn’t that be my choice? Lol

title: Lasts at least 10 hours
text: I got these for my husband for his birthday, and he really loves them. He listens to his music all day, every day at work, and they will last his whole 10 hour shift. Quality and noise cancellation are top notch.

title: Second time I've had to order and appears to be really bad quality.
text: I am very disappointed in that this is the second set of these and with this current set the volume is minimal. Now just this last month only one ear bud will work at a time. This is very disappointing

title: Just so so to me
text: Hard for me to get in and out of holder and my ears I have fat fingers though. Sound quality is so so and do not cancel noise very well. Won’t stay in ear when sleeping either. Not worth the price

title: Buds
text: Fast pairing, good fit excellent sound

title: I wish I could say it was better
text: I wanted to love these but they’re simply not up to par my dad has cheaper ones that are louder and noise cancel more I’m a bit disappointed

title: Amazing!
text: Best headphones and price is reasonable

title: Sounds tinny
text: I bought this for my daughter for Christmas. She say's it sounds terrible. What a waste of $100.00.

title: It's A NO for me..
text: I have only had these for a few days now and am not pleased with the performance of these earbuds.. The battery life is extremely short...may get 4 hours of use. Definitely nowhere close to 8 hours. Also they tend to slip out of my ear. I will admit I have only used the medium and large ear tips, not the small. Not sure if that would make a difference. The sound quality is ok. Nothing to brag about. They are not worth the cost..at all. I was so sure these would work...because they're BEATS but I was sadly disappointed. I'll be returning these.

title: Sounds very clear
text: I love the sound clarity. It’s better than before and I hope they last.

title: So afr so good...except it keeps falling off from my small ear
text:
I own different ANC and so far I like Beats Studio Buds. I love the design and while the case is not as small as Apple Airpod Pro, i'll take it.

ANC
The ANC is somewhat close to Airpod Pro. I would like to run a test in a bust and noisy environment to see how it behave and I will update my post.

So far, this is a win win. Just make sure to select the proper silicon because I have issues when I select small - I have small ear - and it keeps falling off. So, I am eager to see how this thing handle in busy environment like gym.

title: Not for small ears
text:
Sound quality - 4 stars
Pairing - 4 stars
Bass - 4 stars
Fit - 1 star
Noise canceling - 5 stars
I have small ears and these have to be the worst fitting ear buds I’ve ever used. These were an upgrade from my soundcore earbuds but when it comes to fit, sadly they don’t even come close. Sing along with a song, fall out. Turn my head to the side, fall out,l. Walk too hard, fall out. The “twist and lock” design gave me hope, but it was false hope. I’ve tried the original tip and they are too big, the smaller ones “feel” like they fit better, but they just don’t stay in. As if the falling out all the time want enough, I’m either having to constantly readjust to prevent them from falling out or constantly having to put one back in which causes to pause or skip songs.

title: They kept falling out
text: Make sure they’re right for your ears, they kept falling out of mine.

title: AWFUL! DONT BUY THESE
text: If I bought these when they weren’t on sale, I would be very, very angry with the sound quality for the original price they ask. I’m still annoyed I even paid the sale price! They sound like absolute crap in my opinion. Like the 20 dollar ear buds you buy at the gas station. I bought skull candy earbuds from Walmart that were 30 dollars that sounded way better then these!

title: beats buds
text: Sent them back. no way to charge them, NOT user friendly, would not purchase again

title: Most favorite headphones
text: I love these. I got them on sale for $99 and (being the most I've ever spent on headphones) they were worth it. They fit in my small ears just fine and the bass and noise canceling is top.

title: Love THESE!!
text: Love my Beats buds!!! Highly recommend. Only one thing....wish could turn them down on the buds themselves. without having to reach for my phone when I'm in the middle of a workout. It is annoying, especially after having wireless buds before that I could do that with. Sound is fantastic and so is everything else. Fits well. Comfortable.

title: Love them but don’t use noise cancellation
text: I love these. They have good sound quality and battery life. However the noise cancelation causes this weird echoing noise. As soon as I turn it off, it’s fine. I don’t really need it because even with the gyms music on and the noise cancellation off I can still hear my music fine without hearing theirs.

title: Nice but difficult for autistic din
text: These have amazing sound and are a very nice quality item.  Probably not for an individual with autism as these are different than a wired earbud and understanding hue to control them has been difficult even fir a 33 year old that follows directions fairly well.  So…5 stars for the average person.  Not for autism and probably would be difficult fir my parents fir the same reason.  …bought these because of good reviews and the fact I could get a beautiful red!

title: Dies fast
text: Great headphones just dies fast

title: I love it!!!
text:
Amazon delivery prime… the best!!!
About the product, until now, I love it.

title: Do not hesitate!
text: Beats are soo much better then AirPods!

title: Not worth it
text: These are excellent for sitting down and not moving at all that’s about it. They do not stay in your ear and forget about working out or doing any movement with these in as they just fall out and I tried all the different ear pieces. This was a wasted purchase so I am looking to sell after only 2 uses out of them, go with fits for any activities..,

title: Wish I didn’t buy
text: I deliver packages out of a big brown truck, I’ve owned every type of Bluetooth imaginable, airpod, airpod pro, power beats pro, plantronics, and el cheap’os. For what I paid for them they sucked. Battery life sucked. Picking up on speech to text and voice commands sucked. Talking to others sucked. Was constantly falling out of my ears despite switching ear inserts. Hands down the best product out there is the Powerbeats pro, so I assumed these would live up to the beats name, sadly they did not.

title: Auto connect with Iphone
text: I bought them for my IPhone 11 Pro Max. This is not my first Beats product. I didn’t want the long stem from the AirPods. And as always Beats did not disappoint. Also, they automatically connected to my iPhone. I believe this is because Apple purchased Beats.

title: Does not work with Samsung TV
text: These are great earbuds that take advantage of Iphone features just fine but what a disappointment when pairing with my Samsung TV, the volume is so low it makes the buds useless. After researching the issue it turns out to be well known in the Samsung community that this is indeed a problem and has been for sometime, clearly no help is on the way. So if you need to pair with Samsung try another brand of buds. The Sony line works as it should with Samsung.

title: Save your money.
text: Great sound just very bad for answering your phone to talk picks up all the background noise you can't hear the person you're talking too. Battery life is horrible. My 30 dollar ear buds work better

title: They're alright
text: I bought these as a replacement to my AirPods Pro - but they are no replacement. I figured that since Apple owns Beats that they would be comparable, but no such luck. The sound is pretty great, but the mic is not a win. It was okay for me to hear music and phone calls, but nobody could hear me. If you're looking for just listening - they are great!  I now use them to go to the gym - they stay in great, the sound is quality, and as long as I don't have to answer a call, they're amazing. However, I can't use them for work (phone calls and Zoom calls).

title: Great fit and sound
text: Great fit and sound. Easy connection

title: Not real Beats
text: When you try to register the product on the beats website.. it says the serial number is invalid. Also have issues charging them and over a month or 2 of use they don't work as good. Do not recommend

title: Stopped charging and can’t connect to anything after factory reset
text: Stopped charging after 4 months and did a factory reset, now wont connect. POS!!

title: Not as good as a cheaper competitor
text: Still good, but not a great value for the money when less expensive products work just as good.

title: Not loud enough
text: I’ve got an IPhone 13, some of the controls don’t work perfectly as the air buds do. Also it’s not loud enough, they are definitely loud and have good quality sound but just wish it could be louder.

title: Tiny ear holes approved, sounds great
text: Convenient and i could even sleep with em comfy. Sound is great and connectivity is easy. Can connect to multiple devices.

title: Great
text: I love these best purchase I’ve made in a while.

title: Good but glitchy
text: I absolutely love the noise cancelling function and they have the best battery life I've experienced with ear buds. However, they will cut out and do a static "scratch" loudly in my ear. I returned my first pair thinking the issue was a one off, but this pair does the same thing. Also, this pair will suddenly disconnect from my phone, restart, and reconnect all on its own. I initially thought it was for an update or something, but it happens at least twice a week. They are still one of the best earbuds I have had, so I will deal with the issues, but I am slightly disappointed given the cost and brand.

title: An Apple Product...
text:
The sound quality is no better or worse than others in this price class. There is no built in EQ app. Instructions are scant. Meh!

Buy something else!

title: Not very good!!
text: I bought these renewed, they operate and function like new. I’m glad I only paid 64$. I have cheap ear buds that sound better, have better noise cancellation and have great battery life. Tozo NC7 or NC9.  Beats Studio Buds disappointed me ! Apple should be ashamed

title: Fit
text: It doesn’t fit in my ear.. too lose to keep in place

title: The case won’t hold a charge
text: The case won’t hold a charge to keep working

title: Good detail product as described on paper
text: Bad connection on Bluetooth drops within 10 ft or distortion. Can get better response from cheap earbuds  It will only go to like 10 ft.. before distortion.  I am not sure if I am still under the window of sending these back and get the Beats Pro’s. To see if there is a difference. If customer service sees this please contact me. The purpose was for me to go a little bit further than 10ft  to get good sound quality.

title: Complete utter disappointment
text:
The fit very well and are snug.
The sound they make is horrible.
It is mono tone with no bass.
Its not my phone or my pc or any device paired to it, it is they cheap way it is manufactured.
Beats are no longer as good as they use to be.
The app you download does not even have any enhancements and I do not know why
you would even need it as it does nothing but steal data from your phone
This is a gimmick earbud and should be no more than 19.99
